reasoning: The CDC has consistently monitored the ongoing H5N1 outbreaks and, as of early August 2025, confirmed there has been no person-to-person spread globally. While the widespread mammalian outbreak (US dairy cattle) increases the overall background risk compared to previous years, all human infections so far continue to lack evidence of sustained or non-sustained human-to-human transmission. Major suspected events (like the Missouri case with unknown exposure in late 2024) were ruled out via serology. With only ~4.5 months remaining before the resolution at the end of 2025, the time window is quite narrow. Thus, the probability of humans developing human-to-human transmission AND the CDC confirming/reporting it before 2026 is extremely low. I estimate a 3% probability.
